name: Starter Minion / Companion Witch
summary: A minion-oriented Witch starter where summoned bodies absorb pressure and deal damage while the player learns enemy attacks. Beginner-friendly, but minion scaling and Spirit reservation need attention.
who_should_play: New players who want extra bodies between them and danger while learning.
Players who prefer commanding minions over precise aiming.
Players who can tolerate slightly more complex scaling (Spirit, minion stats).
review_notes: Research-backed archetype skeleton. Minion mechanics are patch-sensitive; exact Spirit costs and gem ids pending verified data.

source.notes: Authored from research cards 03-09; not a copied creator build.

failure_modes: Minions die repeatedly if their survivability and your Spirit setup are neglected.
Minion scaling is patch-sensitive; a balance change can shift power noticeably.
Player can become too passive and forget personal defenses.

skills.name: Primary minion skill

supports.name: Minion damage support
supports.note: Core damage scaling for the army.

skills.note: Keep your minion count stable before chasing raw minion damage.

skills.name: Secondary minion or companion
skills.note: A tankier body or utility companion for boss fights.

skills.name: Movement / escape skill
skills.note: You still need to dodge; minions do not make you invincible.

passive_milestones.goal: Take early minion damage and minion life clusters near the Witch start.
Pick up Spirit / reservation efficiency and personal life nodes.
Balance minion survivability with your own defensive masteries before pure damage.

notes: Exact attribute requirements need verified gem and base data before publishing as final.
Minion gems are Intelligence-weighted; some support gems may pull a small Strength or Dexterity requirement.
Minions and persistent companions reserve Spirit; exact costs depend on gem levels and need verified gem data.
Track every reservation so you do not silently run out of Spirit when adding a companion or aura.
Until verified, treat Spirit capacity as the gate on how many minions you can sustain.

weapon.budget_note: A weapon that grants Spirit or +minion levels is worth more than raw spell damage here.

body_armour.base: Energy-shield or hybrid base that meets attribute requirements
body_armour.budget_note: Your own survivability matters; do not rely on minions as your only defense.

helmet.budget_note: A plain rare with life and resists is enough for campaign.

priority: minion or cast modifiers if affordable

boots.base: Any defensive base that meets attribute requirements
boots.budget_note: Movement speed keeps you out of danger while minions fight.

belt.budget_note: Easy slot to patch resistance holes.

amulet.budget_note: The amulet can carry Spirit or Intelligence that keeps your minion setup running.

priority: attributes if a requirement is tight
ring_1.budget_note: Rings are your most flexible resistance slots.

ring_2.budget_note: Pair with ring 1 to finish capping resistances.

gear_swap_warnings.message: If your amulet supplies Spirit or the Intelligence your minion gems need, swapping it without replacing that Spirit/Intelligence can desummon minions or disable a companion.
A weapon that grants Spirit is part of your reservation budget; swapping to a non-Spirit weapon may leave you unable to sustain all minions.

campaign_targets: Keep elemental resistances as high as the campaign allows for your level.
Keep your own life or energy shield current; do not hide behind minions alone.
early_maps_targets: Maintain minion survivability so you are not constantly resummoning under pressure.
defenses.recovery: Plan a personal recovery layer plus minion regeneration/leech where available.
defenses.movement_baseline: Movement speed on boots plus a movement/escape skill.
defenses.chaos_resistance_note: Chaos resistance is a bonus during campaign, not a campaign priority.
map_mods_to_avoid: Not yet verified in-game for this draft; review before mapping.
